examConfigs.title: Technician Exam
examConfigs.description: Technician license review covering rules, repeaters, electronics basics, and station practice.

examConfigs.title: General Exam
examConfigs.description: General license review focused on HF operation, propagation, circuits, and station setup.

examConfigs.title: Amateur Extra Exam
examConfigs.description: Amateur Extra style mixed review using the full question bank for broad concept practice.

questions.prompt: What is the main purpose of a repeater?
What does your amateur call sign do?
What does V = I x R describe?
Which mode is common on local VHF/UHF repeaters?
If frequency increases, wavelength does what?
What does SWR primarily indicate?
Which practice is best before transmitting?
A capacitor stores energy in what?
Which item most directly protects against overcurrent?
Which band type is associated with long-distance skywave communication?
What is the control operator responsible for?
What is the safest action when installing an outdoor antenna?

options.A: Place it close to power lines

questions.prompt: Power is measured in what unit?
A diode primarily does what?
Why might a repeater require a CTCSS tone?
Which statement about low SWR is best?

options.C: Usually indicates a better impedance match

questions.prompt: What is a major use of phonetics on the air?

options.B: Make call signs easier to copy

questions.prompt: Which quantity opposes current flow?
What should you do if you cause interference?
What is one likely risk of replacing a fuse with too high a rating?
Why upgrade from Technician to General?
What often changes HF propagation from day to night?
Which voice mode is commonly used on HF for bandwidth efficiency?
One advantage of CW is what?
What usually happens to coax loss as frequency increases?
Why is a band plan useful?
What is a dummy load used for?
Digital modes can be used for what?
If a 12-volt circuit draws 2 amperes, what power is used?
What is the best response if unsure a transmission is within your privileges?
Which item best helps protect equipment from nearby lightning energy?
What does a transformer commonly do?
Why is listening before calling CQ important?
Which condition most affects VHF/UHF line-of-sight coverage?
What is one common purpose of a tuner or matching network?

options.B: Help match the transmitter system to the load

questions.prompt: Which operating habit is most professional during a busy net?

options.D: Tune up on the active frequency

questions.prompt: What does polarization describe?
What can result from poor grounding or unsafe wiring?
What is most likely to improve weak-signal fixed-station performance?

options.D: Lower the antenna behind a shed

questions.prompt: Why may lower HF bands be preferred at night?
What is the basic role of an antenna?

options.A: Convert RF energy to radio waves and back

questions.prompt: What unit measures resistance?
What does an inductor store energy in?
What is the major hazard when raising an antenna mast?
HF often supports long-distance contacts by what means?
A 10-ohm load with 2 amperes has what voltage?
What does a higher antenna often improve on VHF/UHF?
Why avoid tuning up on an occupied frequency?

options.B: It can interfere with ongoing communications

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes FM on amateur repeaters?
Which practice helps reduce accidental repeater interference?
If current increases in a fixed resistance, voltage does what?
Why might signal reports be weak even with low SWR?

options.A: Antenna placement or efficiency may still be poor

questions.prompt: What is the most responsible action if equipment smells hot?
What is the main purpose of station identification rules?
Which statement about digital communication is true?

options.A: It can include data and weak-signal modes
options.D: It cannot coexist with band planning

questions.prompt: What is one likely result of replacing a fuse with a higher-rated one?

options.C: Higher risk of damage or fire

questions.prompt: What does your call sign identify?
Which mode is narrow bandwidth and useful for weak signals?
What feed line is common for amateur stations?
Which safety rule is most important around outdoor antennas?
What is the usual unit of current?
What is the usual unit of voltage?
What is a resistor used for?
What is a transistor commonly used for?
What is the advantage of a higher-gain antenna?

options.A: It removes the need for ID
options.B: It can concentrate energy in a desired pattern

questions.prompt: What should you do before using a new repeater?
What does line-of-sight mean for VHF/UHF?
What is a common handheld radio antenna tradeoff?
Why is proper grounding important?

options.B: It helps with safety and equipment protection

questions.prompt: What can a dummy load help prevent?
When should you identify your station?
Which part of the spectrum is 2 meters?
What is 70 centimeters commonly classified as?
What happens when feed line length increases?
What is resonance in an antenna system?

options.D: A condition related to efficient operation at a frequency

questions.prompt: What is a primary reason to use phonetics in poor conditions?
What is one benefit of digital weak-signal modes?

options.B: They can decode very weak signals

questions.prompt: What does P = V x I calculate?
If voltage is fixed and resistance increases, current usually does what?
If a station is too close to power lines, what is the best choice?
What is a common reason to use low power on a repeater?

options.A: Use only as much power as needed

questions.prompt: What is one purpose of a band plan on HF?
What is an example of good repeater courtesy?
Why are outdoor feed line connectors often weatherproofed?

options.C: To reduce water intrusion and loss

questions.prompt: What does RF stand for?
What is a likely effect of corroded antenna connections?

options.D: Poor performance and possible intermittent operation

questions.prompt: What is one reason to keep transmissions concise during nets?
What does an antenna tuner mainly tune?

options.C: The impedance relationship seen by the transmitter

questions.prompt: What is the benefit of listening before using a simplex frequency?

options.A: It helps avoid interrupting existing communications

questions.prompt: What is one sign of a poor antenna location?
What does DC mean?
What does AC mean?
What is one reason to avoid overdriving audio on voice modes?

options.C: It can distort your transmitted signal

questions.prompt: What is a practical use of a wattmeter?
What can solar activity affect on HF?
What is one key benefit of an external mobile antenna versus a handheld rubber duck?

options.B: It removes the need for a license

questions.prompt: What is a common cause of reduced battery life in portable operation?

options.C: High transmit power and long transmissions

questions.prompt: What is the first step if a radio suddenly behaves abnormally?
What is one reason CW remains useful?

options.A: It can be effective with weak signals and narrow bandwidth

questions.prompt: What is one reason operators use headsets in noisy environments?
What is the likely result of transmitting outside your privileges?

options.C: Improper operation and possible enforcement issues

questions.prompt: What is one advantage of keeping a simple station log?

options.B: It helps you review contacts and operating issues

questions.prompt: What is one purpose of a net control station?
What is a common sign of overload or interference in nearby electronics?

options.D: Distorted or unintended audio or behavior

questions.prompt: What is one way to reduce accidental on-air interference while testing?

options.B: Use a dummy load when appropriate

questions.prompt: What is one reason to keep antennas away from occupied areas when possible?

options.C: To help manage safety and exposure concerns

questions.prompt: What is one reason to verify polarity before connecting DC equipment?
What is the basic meaning of impedance?

options.D: Opposition to current in AC/RF circuits

questions.prompt: What is one likely result of a poor microphone connection?
What should you do before climbing or servicing a tower or mast system?

options.C: Use proper safety procedures and disconnect hazards

questions.prompt: What is one benefit of using the minimum necessary bandwidth?

options.C: It removes the need for IDs

questions.prompt: What is one common reason to use simplex instead of a repeater?

options.B: Direct station-to-station communication may be sufficient
